provenance.source_corpus: Recovery Patterns of Understory Herbs and Their Use as Indicators of Deciduous Forest Regeneration

source_spans.text: Habitat fragmentation has reduced the richness of native species of forests in northeastern North America.
Habitat fragmentation has reduced the richness of native species of forests in northeastern North America.
species composition was strongly affected by restoration history
species composition was strongly affected by restoration history
intermediately vulnerable VR3 species... showed a significant increase in species richness as time since restoration increased
intermediately vulnerable VR3 species... showed a significant increase in species richness as time since restoration increased
Soil moisture strongly affected the recovery of intermediately vulnerable species.
Soil moisture strongly affected the recovery of intermediately vulnerable species.
recolonization by spring ephemerals seems to be prevented by the dense vegetation cover
recolonization by spring ephemerals seems to be prevented by the dense vegetation cover
because of their limited dispersal ranges, may recolonize restored sites only if they are actively reintroduced.
restored sites in Point Pelée National Park have substantially recovered since the naturalization program was initiated
relatively successful recolonization of restored sites by native species also was likely associated with the proximity of remnant forest habitat
When canopy cover declines with disturbance, ephemerals seem to be replaced
When canopy cover declines with disturbance, ephemerals seem to be replaced
While passive reintroduction will likely facilitate recolonization by vertebrate-dispersed species
For active reintroduction to be successful, however, parallel habitat-level management and restoration activities will be necessary
For active reintroduction to be successful, however, parallel habitat-level management and restoration activities will be necessary
